releaseReview.method: Deterministic editorial repair after a stricter release schema exposed three sentence-boundary defects; no model claim was added.

plan.executiveSummary: Recommend STR-BALANCED for executive consideration—not approval or execution. OBSERVED OTIF is 72.4% versus 84.3% baseline. FACTS: 318 orders and 42 strategic customers are at risk. CALCULATED exposure is $1.24M delayed revenue; ESTIMATED premium-freight exposure is $186K. The SIMULATION reaches 81.2% on Day 14 and 82.2% on Day 21 for $68,750, leaving $6,250 budget headroom. Approval remains pending, and quality, cost-basis, and operating assumptions require checkpoint evidence.
plan.strategyRationale: STR-BALANCED meets both simulated OTIF targets while staying below the $75,000 cap. STR-MARGIN misses both targets; STR-SPEED meets them but leaves only $100 headroom and assumes sustained weekend overtime. Balanced projections are conditional—not causal proof or execution results—and rely on supplier, carrier, and manufacturing recovery assumptions.
evidenceCitations: EVD-PV-001–004: metric completeness, grain, definition consistency, and historical reconciliation passed.
EVD-SUP-017 and EVD-ORD-041: Atlas shortage association; EVD-MFG-009 and EVD-ORD-052: Wilmington delay association.
EVD-LOG-023 and EVD-SHP-012: Delta missed-pickup association; EVD-ORD-077: distributed causes.
EVD-ORD-041 and EVD-CUS-006: 318 at-risk orders and 42 strategic customers.
evidencePacket.strategies and proposedActionGraph: simulated outcomes, $68,750 proposed cost, $6,250 headroom, and human approval pending.

assumptions.statement: The approved alternate supplier covers 38% of the shortage.
Delta pickup acceptance reaches 92% by Day 7.
Wilmington schedule adherence reaches 90% by Day 7.
Quality escape-rate delta remains at or below zero.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Validate the $68,750 bounded-spend basis and approval conditions.
Confirm approved-alternate status, allocation, and 38% coverage evidence.
Validate Wilmington resequencing and 90% adherence feasibility.
Validate bounded Delta capacity and 92% pickup acceptance.
Define Day 7, 14, and 21 evidence checkpoints for OTIF, quality, cost, and leading indicators.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Procurement, manufacturing, and logistics commitments; human approval.

residualRisks: The Day 21 projection has only 0.2 percentage points of target margin; carrier underperformance could erode it.
Quality-constraint compliance is monitored but not demonstrated.
Recovery-cost inputs lack separate source evidence despite internal arithmetic reconciliation.
Contributor shares are observed associations, not exclusive causal proof; 13% remains distributed causes.

findings.title: Target attainment has narrow, assumption-dependent margin
findings.detail: The simulation exceeds the Day 21 target by only 0.2 points. Observed associations do not validate the assumed 38% supplier coverage, 92% pickup acceptance, or 90% schedule adherence.

findings.title: Cost and quality constraints require checkpoint evidence
findings.detail: The arithmetic remains within budget, but source validation of recovery costs and evidence that quality-escape delta remains nonpositive are pending. Existing guards make these conditional rather than blocking defects.

unsupportedClaims: Approved alternate supplier will cover 38% of the shortage.
Delta pickup acceptance will reach 92% by Day 7.
Wilmington schedule adherence will reach 90% by Day 7.
requiredConditions: Executive outcome-owner approval must precede ACT-001 and all downstream commitments.
Finance must validate the $68,750 cost basis and enforce the $75,000 cap before authorization.
Day 7 evidence must validate supplier coverage, pickup acceptance, and schedule adherence; any material shortfall or quality degradation must pause dependent actions and trigger human re-escalation.

counterfactual.scenario: Actual recovery is uniformly 0.3 percentage points below the balanced simulation.
counterfactual.expectedImpact: Projected OTIF becomes 80.9% on Day 14 and 81.9% on Day 21, missing both targets despite remaining within budget.
